mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe economic contraction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and capital demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive return frequency above 55 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand, providing a baseline positive signal for S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50 percent of cases across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215, accelerating inflation forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0 are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in greater than 60 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a liquidity crisis regime indicates severe supply chain disruption and credit contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during liquidity crises fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns with a frequency exceeding 50 percent.
